issues.title: Scaffolder update: stop false-positive drift when a file matches the incoming template
issues.description: `to-execution update` classifies an invariant file as drifted whenever its current hash differs from the recorded stamped-form hash, even when the file is byte-identical to the incoming template (src/bin/cli.js:658 checks current != recorded before comparing current to templateHash). The stale anchor is then carried forward (cli.js:663), so the false positive repeats on every future update. Dogfood test: 8 of 9 reported-drifted files were identical to the template. Fix: when currentHash === templateHash, classify the file unchanged and record templateHash, before the drift check. End-to-end: run update on an Instance whose file lockstep-matches the new template — it reports unchanged, and the rewritten marker anchors the template hash.
acceptance_criteria: A file byte-identical to the incoming template is reported unchanged, never drifted, regardless of the recorded hash
After update, the version marker records the template hash for such files (stale anchor not carried forward)
A file that genuinely differs from both the recorded hash and the template is still reported drifted and left in place
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS on the change
Dogfood root re-run of update reports the 8 previously false-positive files as unchanged
issues.root_cause: update() orders the recorded-hash drift check before the template-content comparison, so lockstep-updated files with a stale anchor are misclassified
issues.fix: update() reordered to test currentHash === templateHash before the recorded-hash drift check: a file byte-identical to the incoming template is classified unchanged and re-anchored to the template hash. Commit 93f1489 (src/bin/cli.js). code-standards + package-qa PASS.
issues.notes: Found dogfood-testing the upgrade path (2026-06-10). The one genuine drift in that test (_teammate.template.md missing the color line) is being caught up directly.

issues.fix: Template ships .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/ via .gitkeep on every init; stamp-policy adds both to WORK_TRACKING_DIR_PREFIXES so update classes them work-tracking and never touches records inside. Commit 7e4a7d8. code-standards + package-qa PASS.
issues.title: Two homes stamped: Template ships .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/, stamp policy classes them work-tracking
issues.description: The Template ships the two Progress Record / Runtime Record homes of ADR-0008: .excn/progress/ (agent- and gate-written) and .excn/runtime/ (hook-written), present on every fresh init. The stamp policy classifies both as work-tracking so update never touches their contents. The stamped .excn gitignore continues to match *_progress.json at any depth with no change. End-to-end: init a temp dir, both homes exist, update leaves files placed in them untouched.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh init stamps .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/
stamp policy classifies both directories as work-tracking (update never refreshes or drift-reports their contents)
Existing *_progress.json gitignore line covers records inside both homes with no gitignore edit
Src-First: lands in src/ first, dogfood root mirrors
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS

issues.fix: The four hook-written Runtime Records move out of the .excn base into .excn/runtime/ (ADR-0008); doctor reads heartbeats + viewer-server state from the new home and lists all 6 features; daemon PATH_WHITELIST serves .excn/runtime/*.json. gate-watch's Progress-Record scan relocates in EXEC-069. Commit 2badcaa (10 files). code-standards PASS (rubric sweep 5f8111f cleared the prior FAIL) + package-qa PASS.
issues.title: Runtime Records move: hooks, doctor, and viewer-server read and write .excn/runtime/
issues.description: Hook-written state becomes Runtime Records: the invocation heartbeat ledger, the viewer-server pid record, and gate-watch state are written to and read from .excn/runtime/. doctor heartbeat checks and the viewer-server daemon read the new home. End-to-end: a hook fires, its invocation lands in .excn/runtime/hook-invocations_progress.json, doctor reports the heartbeat from there.
acceptance_criteria: All hook scripts write their Runtime Records under .excn/runtime/
doctor reads heartbeats and viewer-server state from .excn/runtime/ and reports feature health correctly
No hook writes any *_progress.json at the .excn base
Src-First: template hook sources first, dogfood root mirrors
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS

issues.fix: verdict-ledger.schema.json (new): top-level append-only array of {step, at, artifact, summary}, entry shape under #/$defs/entry; sprint.schema.json step_log references it via cross-file $ref instead of an inlined duplicate; progress.schema.json untouched. ajv-validated against the live session ledger and sprint_6.json. Commit f9f2af6. package-qa PASS (code-standards N/A — schema-only).
issues.title: Verdict Ledger schema: gates' append shape validates strictly
issues.description: A new verdict-ledger schema defines the append-only array of gate verdicts (step, at, artifact, summary). The session Verdict Ledger and a sprint record's step_log entries share the entry shape; the sprint schema references it rather than duplicating it. progress.schema.json remains the unit-of-work tracker shape. End-to-end: the live session ledger and sprint-6 step_log validate strictly against the new schema.
acceptance_criteria: verdict-ledger.schema.json ships in the Template's schemas
session_progress.json validates against it as-is
sprint.schema.json step_log entries reference the same entry shape (no duplicated definition)
progress.schema.json unchanged for trackers
Src-First: template schemas first, dogfood root mirrors

issues.fix: to-execution migrate command relocates legacy flat *_progress.json records into ADR-0008 homes by writer class (runtime basenames -> .excn/runtime/, rest -> .excn/progress/); location-only (fs.rename, byte-identical), idempotent, every move reported; doctor flags legacy layout and names migrate; gate-watch scan follows ledgers into .excn/progress/. Commit 813217e. code-standards + package-qa PASS (24+5-check fixtures). Deferred to Team Lead per the root-restamp ruling: live dogfood-root migration at a clean-tree point, and the durable package-qa rubric extension (alignment-gated).
issues.title: Migrate command relocates Progress and Runtime Records; doctor flags the legacy flat layout
issues.description: A new Scaffolder migrate command relocates known records on an already-stamped Instance into their ADR-0008 homes: versioned migration table, idempotent re-run, location-only (content byte-identical), every move reported. doctor detects the legacy flat layout and names the command. update's never-touch-work-tracking contract is unchanged. End-to-end: a temp Instance with the legacy flat layout migrates cleanly; re-run is a no-op; the dogfood root is migrated as proof.
acceptance_criteria: migrate relocates known *_progress.json records into .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/ by writer class
Re-running migrate is a no-op; file content is byte-identical after relocation
doctor reports the legacy flat layout and names migrate; reports clean after migration
update behavior unchanged (never touches work-tracking)
package-qa rubric extended to exercise the migrate contract (chartered change, alignment-gated per ADR-0004)
Dogfood root migrated: no *_progress.json remains at .excn base; hooks and viewer keep working
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS

issues.fix: PreToolUse deny guard (spawn-guard pattern, ADR-0008): a *_progress.json write outside .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/ is denied with a redirect naming both homes; default OFF, fail-safe, one invocation record per firing, toggle optional in hooks-config.schema.json. Commit e7f4cd5 (10 files). code-standards + package-qa PASS; 15-check payload simulation.
issues.title: Progress-location deny guard: misfiled Progress Record writes are denied with a redirect
issues.description: A PreToolUse guard in the spawn-guard pattern denies a *_progress.json write outside .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/, with a redirect reason naming the right home. Rules/decision split, hooks.config toggle, every firing invocation-logged, fail-safe on any error (a broken guard never blocks legitimate writes). Location only — schema and content stay the gates' job. End-to-end: a simulated misfiled write is denied with the redirect; a correct-home write passes untouched.
acceptance_criteria: Misfiled *_progress.json write denied with a reason naming .excn/progress/ or .excn/runtime/
Writes inside the two homes pass untouched; non-progress writes unaffected
Toggle in hooks.config.json; malformed config or payload fails safe (exit 0, no output)
Every firing logs one invocation record
Payload-simulation tests cover deny, pass, and fail-safe paths
Src-First: template hook source first, dogfood root mirrors; code-standards gate PASSes

issues.fix: Viewer header sprint switcher over probed sprint files (newest first, active default); closed sprints render lanes + decisions + retrospective notes + step_log verdicts; selection round-trips through the History API (?sprint=N deep-link). No server change; read-only. Commit bc8e892. code-standards PASS.
issues.title: Viewer sprint switcher: past sprints readable from the status page
issues.description: The viewer gains a sprint switcher over the sprint files it already probes: the active sprint renders by default; selecting a closed sprint renders the same lanes plus its decisions, retrospective notes, and step_log gate verdicts. End-to-end: sprints 1 through 6 browsable on the live status page.
acceptance_criteria: Switcher lists every probed sprint, newest first, active sprint default
A closed sprint shows lanes, decisions, retrospective notes, and step_log verdicts
No server change; viewer stays read-only over .excn JSON
Src-First: template viewer first, dogfood root mirrors; code-standards gate PASSes; verified on the live viewer server

issues.fix: Viewer hook-health panel over the Runtime Record invocation ledger (.excn/runtime/hook-invocations_progress.json): per-feature doctor-parity cards (firing/stale/disabled + last heartbeat) + collapsible recent-invocations table filterable by feature/outcome; absent/empty ledger renders an explicit empty state; status ledger-derived (mirrors health-policy.js). Bundled the EXEC-067 viewer load-path fix (load_progress.json -> .excn/runtime/). Commit 3bf5bbe. code-standards PASS; verified on the live viewer server.
issues.title: Viewer hook health: doctor-parity cards plus the invocation log
issues.description: The viewer renders per-feature hook health cards mirroring doctor (firing / stale / disabled, last heartbeat) from the Runtime Record invocation ledger, with a collapsible recent-invocations table filterable by feature and outcome. End-to-end: a stale or broken hook is visible on the status page at a glance, with the log entries to diagnose it.
acceptance_criteria: Per-feature cards show doctor-parity status and last heartbeat from .excn/runtime/hook-invocations_progress.json
Collapsible table shows recent invocations, filterable by feature and outcome
Missing or empty ledger renders an explicit empty state, not an error
Src-First: template viewer first, dogfood root mirrors; code-standards gate PASSes; verified on the live viewer server

issues.title: Setup Skill: drop the unused async-questions Preflight gate and sweep stale work-tracking prose
issues.description: The Setup Skill's Preflight hard-gates on the async-questions global skill, but nothing in the product invokes it — it would block every external adopter on a dependency nothing uses. Remove it; make-teammate remains the only global-skill gate. Same sweep: the supporting-info work-tracking line still describes the pre-ADR-0005 layout (all-gitignored, flat) and predates ADR-0008's progress/runtime homes — bring it to current truth. End-to-end: a fresh adopter with only make-teammate installed passes Preflight; the skill's own prose matches the stamped layout.
acceptance_criteria: Preflight requires only make-teammate; async-questions appears nowhere in src/
supporting-info describes committed work-tracking with only *_progress.json ignored (ADR-0005) and the .excn/progress/ + .excn/runtime/ homes (ADR-0008)
Ships in the next npm release
issues.root_cause: Preflight list written before the skill body settled; prose never swept after ADR-0005/0008
issues.fix: Preflight gate on async-questions removed (make-teammate is the only global-skill gate); supporting-info work-tracking prose swept to current truth (ADR-0005 *_progress.json-only ignore + ADR-0008 .excn/progress/ and .excn/runtime/ homes). grep confirms async-questions absent from src/. Commit f8551d8; ships in npm 0.5.0.
issues.notes: Surfaced 2026-06-10 while scoping plugin-marketplace publication: the Preflight dependency story is the real publish blocker.

issues.title: Spawn guard allows persistent team spawns (name + team_name); keeps denying one-shot Teammate copies
issues.description: The spawn guard denies any Agent-tool call whose subagent_type names a rostered persistent Teammate, keying only on AGENT_TYPE_KEYS — it cannot distinguish a transient one-shot copy from a legitimate persistent-teammate spawn carrying name + team_name. Net effect observed live: the Team Lead spawning builder/packager into the team had to fall back to subagent_type general-purpose with the role inlined in the prompt, so rostered Teammates run WITHOUT their .claude/agents definitions. Fix in spawn-guard rules/logic: when the spawn payload carries both name and team_name (a persistent team spawn), allow it even for a rostered type; continue denying bare one-shot spawns of rostered types with the existing redirect reason. Update the deny reason to name the allowed mechanism (spawn with name + team_name into the team).
acceptance_criteria: A spawn payload with subagent_type = rostered Teammate AND both name and team_name passes the guard.
A spawn payload with subagent_type = rostered Teammate and no team_name (or no name) is denied with a redirect reason that names the persistent-spawn mechanism.
Non-rostered types pass unchanged; fail-safe exit 0 on malformed config/payload preserved.
Rules stay data-only (vocabulary in spawn-guard-rules.cjs), decision logic in spawn-guard.cjs.
Verified with payload simulations covering allow/deny/fail-safe; ships as .cjs in template + dogfood mirror; passes code-standards and package-qa gates.
Released to npm in the next version (0.5.2).
issues.root_cause: spawn-guard keyed only on AGENT_TYPE_KEYS, so it could not distinguish a persistent team spawn (name + team_name) from a transient one-shot copy of a rostered Teammate.
issues.fix: Guard allows a rostered type when the payload carries both non-empty name and team_name; one-shots and partial payloads stay denied with a reason naming the mechanism. Commit e5bcf81 after code-standards and package-qa PASS.

issues.title: view-status command: start the viewer server if needed and open the browser to the status page
issues.description: Ship a `to-execution view-status [target]` CLI verb. It checks the viewer-server Runtime Record (.excn/runtime pid record): if the server is running, reuse its port; if absent/stale, start the stamped viewer-server daemon the same way the SessionStart hook does. Then open the user's default browser to the status page URL (macOS `open`, with linux/win fallbacks). Prints the URL either way. Fail-closed CLI semantics (non-zero on a target with no stamped .excn), consistent with existing verbs and usage text; doctor's viewer_server card stays the health authority.
acceptance_criteria: `to-execution view-status` on a stamped Instance with no running server starts the daemon, prints the URL, and opens the browser to it.
With a server already running, it reuses the existing port (no second daemon) and opens the browser.
A stale pid record is treated as not-running (matches doctor's logic).
Non-stamped target exits non-zero with a clear message; usage/help text updated.
Works from the npm-installed package (node_modules/.bin/to-execution view-status).
Passes code-standards and package-qa gates; ships in the next npm version.
issues.fix: view-status verb reuses-or-starts the viewer-server daemon from its pid Runtime Record (doctor-matching staleness) and opens the default browser to the status URL. Commit 487cdcf; gates PASS.

issues.title: validate verb + ajv shipped as a real dependency
issues.description: Gate agents and clerk validate work-tracking JSON against .excn/schemas/ with ajv, but the package ships no validator, so every agent ad-hoc-installs ajv into the host (and npm prunes those modules on the next install — observed on the dogfood root 2026-06-11). Ship `to-execution validate <file> [--schema <path>]` that auto-detects the schema from the file's location/shape (issues partitions, sprint, PRD, progress, hooks config, verdict ledger) and validates with ajv; add ajv (+ ajv-formats if needed) to src/package.json dependencies so the npm install carries it. Exit 0 on valid, non-zero with violation listing on invalid (fail-closed CLI rule).
acceptance_criteria: ajv is a dependency in src/package.json; npm-installed package validates without any ad-hoc install.
`to-execution validate` auto-detects the right schema for backlog/sprint-issues, sprint, PRD, progress, hooks-config, and verdict-ledger files; --schema overrides.
Valid file exits 0; invalid exits non-zero listing each violation with JSON path.
Usage/help text updated; passes code-standards and package-qa gates.
Gate-agent/clerk guidance (CONTEXT or TEAM_DIRECTIVE) points at the verb instead of ad-hoc ajv installs.
issues.root_cause: Package shipped no validator, so gate agents ad-hoc-installed ajv into hosts; the next npm install pruned those modules.
issues.fix: validate verb auto-detects the .excn schema by location/shape and validates with ajv, now a real dependency; TEAM_DIRECTIVE points gates/clerk at the verb. Commit 487cdcf; gates PASS incl. alignment on the directive edit.

issues.title: Stamped hooks break under host "type":"module" — ship template hooks as .cjs
issues.description: All hooks in src/template/.claude/hooks/ are CommonJS .js files. When a host project's package.json declares "type":"module" (observed live in evo-gnit), Node treats every .js hook as ESM and each throws ReferenceError: require is not defined in ES module scope — gate-watch, spawn-guard, message-nudge, viewer-server, load-report, hook-lib, and every rules file silently or visibly fail. Rename the stamped hook and rules files to .cjs, update intra-hook require paths (e.g. require('./hook-lib') → './hook-lib.cjs'), and update every hook command in src/template/.claude/settings.json to point at the .cjs filenames. The Scaffolder must stamp the .cjs layout for new Instances.
acceptance_criteria: Every file in src/template/.claude/hooks/ uses the .cjs extension; no .js hook or rules file remains in the template.
All intra-hook require() paths resolve to the .cjs filenames.
src/template/.claude/settings.json hook commands reference the .cjs paths.
A scaffold stamped into a temp project whose package.json has "type":"module" runs every hook (PostToolUse, Stop, spawn-guard, viewer-server) without an ESM ReferenceError.
package-qa stamp-contract check passes on the new manifest.
issues.root_cause: Stamped hooks were CommonJS .js; host package.json "type":"module" made Node parse them as ESM, so require() threw.
issues.fix: Template hooks renamed to .cjs with settings.json/requires/health-policy updated; landed in commit c624700 after code-standards and package-qa PASS.

issues.title: Migrate already-stamped Instances to the .cjs hook layout
issues.description: Instances stamped before EXEC-075 (e.g. evo-gnit) carry the broken .js hooks and a settings.json pointing at them; under host "type":"module" every hook fails at require(). Extend migrate-policy so the Migrate command renames the stamped hook/rules files to .cjs, rewrites the settings.json hook commands, and removes the stale .js copies. The health check should flag an Instance still on the .js layout.
acceptance_criteria: Running the Migrate command on an Instance stamped with the old .js hooks leaves it on the .cjs layout: hooks renamed, settings.json commands updated, no stale .js hook files.
Migration is idempotent — a second run is a no-op.
Health check reports the .js layout as decayed/migration-due.
Verified end-to-end on evo-gnit: Stop hook runs without the ESM ReferenceError.
issues.root_cause: Instances stamped before EXEC-075 carry CommonJS .js hooks that break under host "type":"module".
issues.fix: migrate renames legacy .js hooks to .cjs (hash-guarded, idempotent, marker re-keying), rewrites settings.json commands and sibling requires; doctor flags .js layout MIGRATION-DUE. Landed in commit 7b54a6e after code-standards and package-qa PASS; verified live on evo-gnit.
